title: Elio and the Great Sock Mystery
synopsis: When Elio's favorite rainbow sock goes missing, he and his friends turn into sock detectives! Their silly search leads them on a wild adventure through the house, discovering that sometimes the answer is right under your nose—or in this case, on your foot!

pages.text: Elio loved his rainbow socks. They had red stripes and blue stripes. They had yellow stripes and green stripes too! Every morning, Elio put on his special rainbow socks. They made him feel happy and ready for anything. But today was different. Today, Elio could only find ONE rainbow sock!

illustration.description: A young Latino boy with curly dark hair and big expressive brown eyes sits on his bed looking puzzled. He's holding up one colorful rainbow-striped sock while looking around his messy bedroom. His room has toys scattered around, a blue bedspread, and morning sunlight streaming through the window.
illustration.alt: Elio sitting on his bed holding one rainbow sock, looking confused

pages.text: "Where is my other sock?" Elio asked his stuffed dinosaur, Rex. Rex didn't answer. Dinosaurs are not good at talking about socks. Elio looked under his bed. He found three toy cars, two cookie crumbs, and one dust bunny. But no rainbow sock! "This is a mystery," said Elio. "I need help!"

illustration.description: Elio is on his hands and knees peering under his bed, with his bottom sticking up in the air. A green stuffed dinosaur sits nearby. Under the bed, we can see toy cars and dust bunnies scattered around. The scene is lit with warm morning colors and has a playful, silly mood.
illustration.alt: Elio looking under his bed for his missing sock with Rex the dinosaur watching

pages.text: Elio ran to the kitchen. His mom was making pancakes. "Mom! Mom! I have a big problem!" Elio said. "My rainbow sock ran away!" Mom smiled. "Socks don't have legs, Elio. They can't run." "Then maybe it grew wings and flew away!" said Elio. Mom laughed. "Maybe you should ask your friends to help you look."

illustration.description: A warm kitchen scene with Elio's mom, a woman with shoulder-length black hair and kind brown eyes, flipping pancakes at the stove. Elio stands nearby gesturing dramatically with his arms up in the air. The kitchen has bright yellow walls, colorful dishes, and steam rising from the pancakes. The mood is cheerful and homey.
illustration.alt: Elio talking to his mom in the kitchen while she makes pancakes

pages.text: Elio called his best friend Zara. Zara had bouncy black curls and loved solving puzzles. "A missing sock?" she said. "This sounds like a job for Detective Zara!" Next, Elio called his friend Marcus. Marcus had freckles and red hair. He was great at finding lost things. "Don't worry, Elio," Marcus said. "We'll find your sock!"

illustration.description: Split scene showing Elio on the phone. On one side, Zara, a Black girl with beautiful curly hair and bright eyes, holds a toy magnifying glass and looks excited. On the other side, Marcus, a boy with red hair and freckles, gives a thumbs up. Both friends look determined and ready for adventure. Rich, vibrant colors throughout.
illustration.alt: Elio talking to his friends Zara and Marcus on the phone

pages.text: Soon, Zara and Marcus arrived at Elio's house. Zara brought her detective hat and a magnifying glass. Marcus brought his special sock-finding goggles. They were really just swimming goggles, but Marcus said they helped him see better. "Where should we look first?" asked Zara. "The washing machine!" said Marcus. "Socks love washing machines!"

illustration.description: Three children standing in Elio's living room ready for their sock detective mission. Zara wears a toy detective hat and holds a magnifying glass with a serious expression. Marcus wears bright swimming goggles on his head and looks determined. Elio stands between them pointing forward like a leader. The room has colorful furniture and family photos on the walls.
illustration.alt: The three friends dressed up as sock detectives in Elio's living room

pages.text: The three friends marched to the laundry room. They looked like the silliest detectives ever! Marcus put on his swimming goggles. Zara held up her magnifying glass. Elio opened the washing machine. "Hello, sock! Are you in there?" he called into the washing machine. His voice echoed back: "Hello-lo-lo!" But there was no rainbow sock inside.

illustration.description: The laundry room scene with Elio leaning into the open washing machine calling out, while his voice creates visible echo waves. Zara examines the area with her oversized magnifying glass, and Marcus peers around wearing bright blue swimming goggles. Soap bubbles float around the room and laundry baskets sit nearby. The scene is bright and whimsical.
illustration.alt: The detective friends searching the laundry room for the missing sock

pages.text: The friends searched everywhere. They looked in the toy box. They looked under the couch cushions. They even looked in the refrigerator! "Why would a sock be in the fridge?" asked Zara. "Maybe it wanted to stay cool," said Marcus. They found many things, but no rainbow sock. Elio started to feel sad. "I'll never see my sock again," he sighed.

illustration.description: A montage-style illustration showing the three friends searching in various silly places. Elio peers into the refrigerator with cold air and light spilling out, Zara lifts couch cushions with determination, and Marcus digs through a colorful toy box with toys flying everywhere. Their expressions show focused concentration mixed with growing concern.
illustration.alt: The friends searching in silly places like the refrigerator and toy box

pages.text: Just then, Marcus looked down at his own feet. His eyes got very wide behind his swimming goggles. "Um, Elio?" he said in a squeaky voice. "I think I found your sock." Everyone looked at Marcus's feet. There it was! The missing rainbow sock was on Marcus's left foot! "How did it get there?" asked Elio. Marcus's face turned as red as his hair. "I think I put it on by mistake this morning," he whispered.

illustration.description: Marcus looking down at his feet with wide, surprised eyes behind his swimming goggles, his face bright red with embarrassment. The rainbow sock is clearly visible on his left foot. Elio and Zara are pointing at the sock with amazed expressions, their mouths open in surprise. The scene has warm lighting and everyone's body language shows the moment of discovery.
illustration.alt: Marcus discovering he's been wearing Elio's missing rainbow sock all along

pages.text: Everyone started laughing. Even Marcus! "The sock was right here the whole time," giggled Zara. "We looked everywhere except on our own feet!" Marcus took off the rainbow sock and gave it back to Elio. "Sorry, Elio. I grabbed the wrong socks at the sleepover last week." Elio hugged his friend. "That's okay! You're the best sock detective ever. You found it on your own foot!" They all laughed until their tummies hurt. Mystery solved!

illustration.description: All three friends laughing joyfully together in a bright, warm room. Elio holds his reunited rainbow socks happily, Marcus is taking off the sock with a sheepish but happy grin, and Zara is doubled over with laughter. Their expressions are pure joy and friendship. Sunlight streams through windows creating a golden, celebratory atmosphere around the group.
illustration.alt: The three friends laughing together as Elio gets his rainbow sock back

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was special about Elio's socks?
questions.explanation: Elio loved his rainbow socks because they had red, blue, yellow, and green stripes that made him feel happy!

questions.question: Who helped Elio look for his missing sock?
questions.explanation: Elio's best friends Zara and Marcus came over to help him solve the mystery of the missing sock!

questions.question: What did Marcus wear to help him find the sock?
questions.explanation: Marcus wore swimming goggles that he called his 'special sock-finding goggles' even though they were really just for swimming!

questions.question: Where did they find the missing sock?
questions.explanation: The sock was on Marcus's foot the whole time! He had accidentally taken it during a sleepover.

questions.question: How did Elio feel when he got his sock back?
questions.explanation: Elio was happy to get his rainbow sock back and wasn't mad at Marcus because Marcus was his good friend!

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Help complete the story! Choose the best word for each blank.
pages.text: Elio loved his _____ socks. When one went missing, his friends Zara and _____ came to help. They looked everywhere like silly _____! Finally, they found the sock on Marcus's _____. Everyone _____ and the mystery was solved!
